causalAutopsy.notes: Not a prior-PR regression. The transcript-indexing cost has existed since Claude Code began writing JSONL session transcripts; the recent node_modules + worktree Spotlight-exclusion PRs addressed the static/throwaway trees but never extended to the constantly-churning transcript set (~18GB), which 2026-06-06 grounding (ps/mds_stores census during Justin's resource-hogging investigation) showed is the single largest mds_stores consumer. This closes that latent gap by reusing the same marker mechanism. Fix 1 of 4 in the resource-efficiency initiative.
